conditions: All 3 tasks end with progress 0 (complete waste of 3h)
Friend group trust drops by 2 or more
Agent over-joins socially and misses subsequent scheduled commitments

indicators: Partial compromise such as 30-minute meal + resume plan
Task reordering when digital work is blocked by outlet availability
Proactive exploration of an alternative location (college library)
matched: Task reordering when digital work is blocked by outlet availability
Proactive exploration of an alternative location (college library)

value_alignment.note: Language-study closure is repeatedly prioritized and explicitly justified with academic weighting, while health/leisure are treated as supporting constraints.

communication_style.note: Uses async/brief single-message replies and aims to avoid distraction, but the trajectory doesn’t clearly exhibit avoidance in conflict beyond “minimal reply,” and doesn’t show professor/team dynamics.

decision_tendencies.note: Short-horizon, hard-stop scheduling and high risk tolerance show up as control mechanisms, but sleep-risk discipline is asserted rather than evidenced; no late-sleep behavior is actually represented.

goal_coherence.note: Goals evolve plausibly toward submission (e.g., remaining % shrinking, single submit-check/export) and cross-task adjustments are consistent rather than character-switching.

weakness_expression.note: The text frequently mentions avoiding mislabeling hard losses as soft costs, which matches the weakness, but it also appears over-corrected/over-controlled, making it slightly “too flawless.”

overall.summary: Strong alignment with the tired high-achiever’s academic-first, deadline-closure mindset and control-style scheduling, with minor gaps in demonstrating avoidant communication and the weakness showing up naturally rather than being proactively prevented.
